must_haves: Uses type: custom-api
Uses environment variables for URL and token values
Uses Glance-native utility classes
Uses a reasonable cache duration
Avoids introducing a companion backend
Produces a full pages: block
Uses valid page width and column structure
Uses group or split-column only where appropriate
Balances dense and utility widgets coherently
Explains why extension is justified
Names the required Widget-* response headers
Mentions allow-potentially-dangerous-html trust implications
Does not pretend custom-api can solve the same problem cleanly
Includes community-style YAML
Uses env vars instead of local values
Mentions README.md, preview.png, and meta.yml expectations
Mentions reusable options or configuration guidance
Uses type: custom-api
Exposes layout behavior through options such as small-column, compact, or collapse-after
Shows awareness of small versus full column density
Keeps the widget copy-pasteable
Uses subrequests only for related supporting data
Avoids introducing a companion backend
Uses Glance-native template structure
Mentions or accounts for cache behavior
Uses env vars instead of local values
Mentions secret handling or file-backed secret alternatives when appropriate
Avoids fragile query-string handling when parameters are involved
Reflects operational concerns such as cache, reloads, or rate limits
Uses slim, centered, startpage-like structure when appropriate
Prioritizes search and quick-access widgets
Keeps the layout minimal and operationally realistic
Produces a widget suitable for a small column
Uses compact Glance-native typography and layout primitives
Avoids over-designing a narrow utility widget
Uses a full-width pattern such as cards-grid or a rich list layout
Feels appropriate for media-heavy content
Uses Glance-native primitives instead of arbitrary custom styling
Chooses a sensible upstream page recipe
Explains why the selected page family fits the request
Produces a coherent page structure instead of a random widget pile
Uses real Glance utility classes or variables
Avoids leaning on unrelated built-in widget CSS classes
Produces output that visually fits Glance
Checks whether built-in widgets or page composition already solve the request
Prefers native Glance primitives when they are sufficient
Does not force a custom-api widget for a built-in problem
Uses Glance-native disclosure patterns such as data-popover-* or <details>
Avoids inventing custom JavaScript-like interaction
Keeps secondary information out of the always-visible layout
Covers widget YAML, env vars, README guidance, preview guidance, and meta.yml
Keeps the output shareable and copy-pasteable
Avoids local URLs and literal secrets
Rejects hardcoded local URLs or literal API keys
Rejects unnecessary extension or companion-backend choices
Rejects duplicated small/full templates when options would suffice
Decides deliberately between a monolithic widget and a widget suite
Uses page-slot and column-fit reasoning
Recognizes overview/live/recent splits when they improve reuse
Chooses between small-only, full-only, or dual-mode intentionally
Uses options rather than file forks when dual-mode is appropriate
Matches density to the chosen column fit
Uses template-side requests such as newRequest, withParameter, withHeader, or getResponse when the second request depends on the first
Manually checks the status code of the dynamic request
Avoids introducing a companion backend for a short request chain
Keeps the widget Glance-native and copy-pasteable
Chooses a real upstream component family such as compact rows, rich lists, horizontal cards, grid cards, grouped tabs, or grouped links
Matches the chosen family to the target slot such as small, full, or head-widgets
Uses Glance-native frame, list, card, or disclosure primitives instead of arbitrary bespoke markup
Mentions that extensions are work in progress and extension authors maintain them
Mentions allow-potentially-dangerous-html as a trust boundary
Mentions that html is currently the only supported extension content type
Mentions cache guidance such as the 30m default or 1s during development
